description: Claims manifest for the Atmosphere 4 blog post. Each entry is a load-bearing public claim mapped to greppable code evidence and (optionally) Maven Central artifacts that must exist at the released version. Consumed by scripts/validate-blog-claims.sh. Seeded from the release-gap matrix that reproduced the 4.0.59 incident: the blog was audited against main and passed while the released artifact lacked these claims (worst: the step-one dependency atmosphere-ai-spring-boot-starter had never been published to Central).
rules: Evidence symbols must match a NON-comment, non-import source line (the validator strips lines starting with *, //, /*, import, or <!--). Evidence paths must never point into .harness/ — a manifest citing itself is the self-satisfying-gate failure mode this repo was burned by.

claims.claim: One Maven dependency gets you started: org.atmosphere:atmosphere-ai-spring-boot-starter (the blog's step-one entry point)
@AiEndpoint(broadcastReply = true) fan-outs the agent reply to the room
A skill's '## Guardrails' section is enforced as a runtime scope policy, not just prompt text
@AgentScope on a @Coordinator class is honored (scope policy built at processing time)
Multimodal (audio) input rides the streaming session via stream(message, parts)
OpenClaw workspace extension files are read and applied to the agent runtime
Cedar policies are enforced through the real cedar CLI (mandatory --entities argument supplied)
The Postgres audit sink persists decisions with a real JSONB cast, not a stringly bind
The admin bundle self-wires a default CheckpointStore (@ConditionalOnMissingBean) via its atmosphere-checkpoint dependency
Durable agent runs are wired into the live request path (spine consumed by the endpoint handler, installed by autoconfig)
